Unlike standard queries, RAG queries have a unique property of context reuse where the same documents recur across user queries. Thus, fully recomputing documents for every RAG query does redundant compute and increases TTFT. Prior works precompute KV tensors of RAG documents offline and coarsely recompute some tokens during online prefill. However, such KV reuse is often slower than full recomputation on modern GPUs due to high-latency disk transfers. Further, such a coarse-grained recomputation degrades accuracy.
  To address these limitations, this paper proposes SIFT: Selective-Index For Fast Compute of RAG Prefill by Exploiting Attention Invariance. SIFT processes documents offline and extracts fine-grained locations of high attention scores for each document. Next, we identify the following attention invariance insights that enable us to exploit the extracted locations during runtime: (1) Local-Attention Invariance: The location of high attention scores within a document remain invariant to surrounding documents. This helps us predict the location of high scores where the document attends to itself. (2) Cross-Attention Consistency: Keys with high intra-document attention also attract cross-attention from subsequent documents. This helps us predict the location of high scores where the document attends to future documents. Critically, SIFT stores no KV data and only stores locations of high scores in the form of two compact bit vectors. SIFT's storage is up to 24,000x smaller than KV tensors, obviating costly disk transfers. During prefill, SIFT computes the attention only for the marked locations and improves TTFT by 1.71x while holding accuracy within 1% of full recompute.

AI.tldr: 提出SIFT方法，通过离线识别文档中高注意力分数的位置（而非存储KV张量），在RAG预填充阶段只计算这些标记位置的注意力，从而减少冗余计算、降低首 token 延迟（TTFT）并保持精度。
AI.motivation: RAG查询中上下文会被复用，但现有方法全量重计算KV或粗糙重利用KV张量存在冗余计算、高延迟磁盘传输和精度下降问题，亟需一种高效且精确的预填充加速方案。
AI.method: 离线提取每个文档中高注意力分数的细粒度位置，利用两个关键洞察：（1）局部注意力不变性：文档内高注意力位置不受周围文档影响；（2）跨注意力一致性：高内注意力键也吸引跨文档注意力。仅存储两个紧凑位向量（比KV张量小24000倍），在线预填充时只计算标记位置的注意力。
AI.result: TTFT提升1.71倍，精度保持在完全重计算的1%以内，存储开销比KV张量小24000倍，避免昂贵的磁盘传输。
AI.conclusion: SIFT通过细粒度注意力位置选择实现了RAG预填充的加速，兼顾计算效率与精度，解决了现有KV重用方法在存储传输和精度上的双重瓶颈。

title: In-Context Learning for the Imputation of Public Opinion Data with Large Language Models
summary: Large language models have been widely evaluated as simulators of individual survey responses. In practice, however, fully unobserved responses are rare; the dominant problem is partial non-response. Imputation aims to restore the overall structure of a survey dataset by filling in these missing values. It has its own well-defined evaluation criteria and differs fundamentally from prediction. We propose to impute missing survey data through in-context learning (ICL). We systematically evaluate ICL design choices across different missingness mechanisms (MCAR, MAR, MNAR) on 150 opinion variables spanning 15 waves of the American Trends Panel. Compared to well-established statistical methods for data imputation like MICE PMM, our ICL approach consistently reduces absolute error across all missingness mechanisms, with the largest gains under non-random missingness (MNAR). Notably, the best-performing specification (gpt-oss-120b with 100 in-context examples) achieves near-nominal aggregate coverage (approaching the 95% level) with confidence intervals two to five times narrower than MICE PMM. We publish a Python package with an sklearn-like API to enable easy deployment of our method using local and proprietary LLMs.

AI.tldr: 本文提出使用大语言模型的上下文学习（ICL）方法进行调查数据缺失值插补，在150个变量、15波次的数据上，相比传统统计方法MICE PMM，ICL方法在所有缺失机制下均降低了绝对误差，尤其在非随机缺失（MNAR）下提升最大，且最佳模型实现了接近95%的覆盖率与更窄的置信区间。
AI.motivation: 实际调查中完全无响应的数据很少见，主要问题是部分无响应（缺失数据）。传统插补方法（如MICE PMM）在处理缺失数据时仍有改进空间，尤其是非随机缺失场景。作者希望利用LLM的上下文学习能力来更准确地插补缺失的调查数据。
AI.method: 采用上下文学习（ICL）方法，利用大语言模型基于已有数据上下文来预测缺失值。在150个意见变量、15波次美国趋势面板数据上，系统评估了不同ICL设计选择，并对比了三种缺失机制（MCAR、MAR、MNAR）下的表现。
AI.result: ICL方法在所有缺失机制下均优于MICE PMM，绝对误差更低。在MNAR下增益最大。最佳配置（gpt-oss-120b，100个上下文示例）实现了接近95%的名义覆盖率，且置信区间比MICE PMM窄2到5倍。
AI.conclusion: LLM的上下文学习是一种有效的调查数据缺失值插补方法，特别是针对非随机缺失场景。作者发布了兼容sklearn API的Python包，便于使用本地和商业LLM部署该方法。

title: GENERIC-FNO: Embedding Energy Conservation and Entropy Production into Fourier Neural Operators
summary: We introduce GENERIC-FNO, the first neural operator to embed the full GENERIC (metriplectic) structure of nonequilibrium thermodynamics -- reversible, energy-conserving dynamics and irreversible, entropy-producing dynamics coupled through the degeneracy conditions -- directly in function space. Existing structure-preserving neural operators enforce at most a single conservation law or reversible (Hamiltonian) structure, while thermodynamically consistent learning has been confined to finite-dimensional, graph, or particle systems. GENERIC-FNO closes this gap: it learns the energy and entropy functionals as neural operators and parameterizes the Poisson and friction operators as diagonal Fourier multipliers sandwiched between rank-one projections that enforce the degeneracy conditions exactly, by construction, with no penalty term, update projection, or residual. The degeneracy identities hold to machine precision (residuals ~10^-13) for any initialization, dimension, or resolution, so the continuous-time dynamics conserve the learned energy and produce entropy exactly; the explicit time stepping adds only a small O(dt^2) drift (per-step residual ~10^-6). We further note that the (E,S,L,M) decomposition of a given flow is not unique, and introduce a gauge-invariant dissipation diagnostic separating reversible from dissipative dynamics independently of the learned functionals. Across three operator backbones (1D/2D FNOs and DeepONet) and four PDEs spanning reversible, dissipative, and mixed regimes, GENERIC-FNO preserves its exact structural guarantees zero-shot across a 4x super-resolution range (64 to 256), recovers the ground-truth ordering of physical dissipation, and is competitive with strong unconstrained and energy-penalized baselines, outperforming them on several dissipative and mixed problems at comparable or fewer parameters.

AI.tldr: GENERIC-FNO是首个将非平衡热力学的完整GENERIC结构（可逆+不可逆动力学）嵌入函数空间的神经算子，通过傅里叶乘子和秩一投影精确满足退化条件，在多种PDE上实现了零样本超分辨率且与强基线竞争。
AI.motivation: 现有结构保持的神经算子最多只能强制执行单一守恒律或可逆（哈密顿）结构，而热力学一致的学习局限于有限维、图或粒子系统，无法在函数空间处理兼具可逆与不可逆动力学的偏微分方程。
AI.method: 将能量和熵泛函学习为神经算子；将泊松算子和摩擦算子参数化为对角傅里叶乘子，两端夹持秩一投影以精确强制执行退化条件，无需惩罚项、更新投影或残差。
AI.result: 退化恒等式在任意初始化/维度/分辨率下达到机器精度（残差~10^-13）；时间离散仅引入O(dt^2)漂移（每步残差~10^-6）；在4倍超分辨率（64→256）下零样本保持结构保证；在多个耗散和混合问题上以更少参数超越强基线。
AI.conclusion: GENERIC-FNO成功弥合了热力学一致学习在函数空间的空白，为兼具可逆与不可逆动力学的PDE学习提供了精确结构保持的新范式，并引入了规范不变的耗散诊断工具。

title: AliyunConsoleAgent: Training Web Agents in Real-World Cloud Environments via Distillation and Reinforcement Learning
summary: We present AliyunConsoleAgent, a web agent framework for automated documentation verification in real-world cloud consoles. Major cloud platforms encompass hundreds of products with rapid feature iteration, causing console UIs to frequently diverge from their corresponding documentation. Verifying that documented procedures accurately reflect the current console and can be executed end-to-end demands an estimated 4 million recurring inspections annually, yet manual coverage remains below 1%. While agent systems built on frontier proprietary models achieve high success rates, their prohibitive cost and data privacy constraints preclude large-scale deployment. We propose a two-stage training paradigm: supervised fine-tuning (SFT) on distilled frontier-model trajectories, followed by reinforcement learning using Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) and a dual-channel outcome reward model in real cloud environments. To support large-scale RL training, we construct a high-determinism rollout system featuring Terraform-based resource pre-provisioning and LLM-driven on-demand provisioning, which effectively isolates environment noise from the training signal. We further introduce a rule-based reward evaluation protocol grounded in backend audit logs, providing objective, reward-hacking-resistant outcome judgment. Our model evolves from mechanical instruction following to autonomous decision-making with cloud console and product-specific understanding. Experiments on a challenging 278-task benchmark where the best frontier model achieves only 65.34% demonstrate that AliyunConsoleAgent-32B achieves a 63.52% mean success rate -- a 20.24 percentage-point improvement over the base model, narrowing the gap to the best frontier proprietary model to 1.82 pp (bootstrap 95% CI [-1.27, 7.39]) -- at 92% lower inference cost.

AI.tldr: 阿里云提出AliyunConsoleAgent框架，通过两阶段训练（SFT + GRPO强化学习）在真实云控制台上自动化文档验证，以32B模型实现接近前沿闭源模型的性能（差距1.82pp），但推理成本降低92%。
AI.motivation: 云平台拥有数百个产品且功能迭代频繁，控制台UI经常与文档不一致。人工验证覆盖率不足1%，而前沿闭源模型虽效果好但成本高昂且存在数据隐私问题，无法大规模部署。
AI.method: 两阶段训练范式：1) 在蒸馏的前沿模型轨迹上进行监督微调（SFT）；2) 使用GRPO和双通道结果奖励模型在真实云环境中进行强化学习。构建了基于Terraform的高确定性rollout系统和基于后端审计日志的规则化奖励评估协议。
AI.result: 在278任务基准（最佳闭源模型仅65.34%）上，AliyunConsoleAgent-32B达到63.52%平均成功率，比基模型提升20.24pp，与最佳闭源模型差距仅1.82pp（95%置信区间[-1.27, 7.39]），推理成本降低92%。
AI.conclusion: 该工作证明了通过两阶段训练（SFT+RL）和精心设计的奖励机制，较小模型可以在特定领域任务上以极低成本接近甚至媲美前沿闭源模型的性能，为大规模自动化文档验证提供了可行方案。

title: Is Text All You Need? Text as a Universal Information Bottleneck for Speech LLMs
summary: Large language models (LLMs) provide a powerful reasoning backbone for speech understanding, but integrating continuous acoustic signals into a frozen LLM remains challenging. Existing speech-to-LLM interfaces typically operate at two extremes: either enforcing near-discrete token alignment, which benefits transcription but loses paralinguistic information, or learning unconstrained continuous representations, which can drift away from the LLM's input space and degrade autoregressive decoding. In this work, we propose Convex Gate (C-Gate), a speech-to-LLM bridge that constrains all speech representations to lie within the LLM's input embedding manifold with an architectural convex-hull constraint. Concretely, each frame is represented as a convex combination of token embeddings, ensuring compatibility with the pretrained LLM while preserving continuous expressivity. Across automatic speech recognition (ASR) and emotion recognition, C-Gate achieves strong joint performance, improving LibriSpeech WER by up to 48.7% relative while matching or exceeding single-task emotion accuracy. Beyond performance, our analysis reveals a key insight: information is not carried by discrete token identities, but by time-resolved trajectories in the embedding space. Causal interventions confirm that both the trajectory structure and alignment to the pretrained embedding manifold are critical for performance. These results suggest that geometry, rather than token discreteness, is the fundamental design factor in speech-to-LLM interfaces, and provide a controlled regime for studying multimodal integration in frozen LLMs. We release the checkpoint, per-sample outputs, mechanism dumps, and intervention suite for replication.

AI.tldr: 提出了Convex Gate (C-Gate)，一种将连续语音信号映射到冻结LLM嵌入流形凸包内的接口，通过凸组合约束保持连续表达性同时兼容LLM，在ASR和情感识别上表现优异，揭示了几何结构而非离散token是关键设计因素。
AI.motivation: 现有语音到LLM接口存在两难：要么强制离散token对齐（丢失副语言信息），要么学习无约束连续表示（偏离LLM输入空间导致自回归解码退化）。需要一种既能保持连续表达性又能与预训练LLM兼容的桥接方法。
AI.method: 提出Convex Gate (C-Gate)，将每个语音帧表示为LLM token嵌入的凸组合（convex combination），确保所有语音表示都位于LLM输入嵌入流形的凸包内，通过架构级的凸包约束实现兼容性与连续性的平衡。
AI.result: 在LibriSpeech ASR上WER相对降低最高48.7%，同时在情感识别上达到或超过单任务准确率。分析发现信息由嵌入空间中的时间分辨轨迹承载，而非离散token身份。
AI.conclusion: 几何结构（而非token离散性）是语音到LLM接口的核心设计因素，C-Gate为冻结LLM中的多模态融合提供了可控的研究范式。

title: Generative Frontier Planning for Adaptive Peer-Referral Recruitment under Covariate-Dependent Arrivals
summary: Peer-referral recruitment systems such as respondent-driven sampling are critical for studying and intervening on hidden populations affected by infectious diseases. To accelerate recruitment, public health agencies must adaptively allocate limited referral resources across multiple rounds, where current decisions shape both the number and the covariates of future recruits. Prior work makes this problem tractable by assuming that referrals are drawn i.i.d.\ from a homogeneous population, an assumption that ignores the homophily and shared context that drive real peer recruitment. We instead consider a more realistic model in which both referral capacity and the covariates of newly referred individuals are conditioned on the referrer, learned from data with a censored count model and a conditional generative model. The resulting planning problem is challenging because each candidate allocation induces a different distribution over future recruits. We propose \emph{Generative Frontier Planning} (GFP), a model-based planner that replaces per-step Monte-Carlo sampling with a deterministic backup over a latent covariate-coverage value surrogate. The surrogate is designed so that the expected value of the next frontier depends on the offspring generative model only through finite-dimensional summaries that are amortized offline, and so that the resulting per-round objective is monotone with diminishing returns. Together, these two properties make planning tractable: the deterministic backup eliminates Monte-Carlo sampling, and the diminishing-returns structure lets a marginal greedy allocation achieve a \((1-1/e)\)-approximation for the per-round problem. On a simulation environment calibrated to a real respondent-driven sampling dataset, GFP outperforms random, reinforcement-learning, and i.i.d.\ dynamic-programming baselines across four discount factors.

AI.tldr: 本文针对隐藏人群的同伴推荐招募问题，提出了一种考虑推荐者异质性和同质性的生成前沿规划方法（GFP），通过离线学习条件生成模型和边际贪心分配策略，在真实校准的仿真环境中优于随机、强化学习和独立同分布动态规划基线。
AI.motivation: 现有同伴推荐招募研究假设被推荐者独立同分布地来自同质人群，忽略了现实中的同质性（homophily）和共享背景。这种假设导致资源分配策略不准确。本文动机是在更真实的模型下，考虑推荐者条件分布，设计可解的规划方法。
AI.method: 提出Generative Frontier Planning (GFP)方法：1）使用删失计数模型和条件生成模型从数据中学习推荐者条件分布；2）设计一个潜在协变量覆盖的价值代理函数，使得每轮期望值仅通过离线摊销的有限维摘要依赖于生成模型；3）利用该代理函数的单调性与边际效益递减性质，用边际贪心分配实现(1-1/e)近似保证，替代蒙特卡洛采样。
AI.result: 在基于真实同伴推荐招募数据校准的仿真环境中，GFP在四种折扣因子下均优于随机分配、强化学习和独立同分布动态规划基线方法。
AI.conclusion: 通过充分考虑推荐者的条件异质性并设计具有理论保证的高效规划方法，可以显著提升隐藏人群同伴招募的资源分配效率，为现实公共卫生干预提供可行的决策工具。

title: TheoremBench: Evaluating LLMs on Theorem Proving in Formal Mathematics
summary: LLMs have recently achieved strong results on formal proving benchmarks. However, existing evaluations remain heavily concentrated on competition-style problems and often fail to capture how models behave on longer, more dependency-rich mathematical developments. We introduce TheoremBench, a Lean4 benchmark designed to evaluate theorem provers beyond contest settings. The benchmark is built from nearly one hundred classical theorems and is released in two complementary forms: a plain main version containing one target theorem per instance, and a premised version that expands each theorem into a structured family of related proving tasks consisting of the main theorem together with automatically extracted supporting subtheorems. This design enables evaluation of not only whether the final theorem was proved from scratch, but also of partial progress through the internal proof structure of a theorem. Our experiments show that explicit premises substantially improve performance for Lean4-capable prover models. To provide a comprehensive evaluation, we introduce theorem-level coverage and token-efficiency metrics that expose qualitative differences in proof behavior. The results show that current provers remain strongly biased toward easy subtheorems and often solve theorems through long and inefficient tactic traces rather than compact proof plans. TheoremBench therefore provides a more fine-grained view of formal reasoning ability and highlights the importance of structural benchmark design for evaluating Lean4 theorem provers.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了TheoremBench，一个基于Lean4的定理证明基准测试，包含近100个经典定理，以plain和premised两种形式发布。实验表明，显式前提显著提升了证明器的性能，并揭示了当前证明器在细粒度推理能力和证明效率上的不足。
AI.motivation: 现有LLM定理证明评估过度集中在竞赛类问题上，缺乏对更复杂、依赖关系更强的数学发展的测试，因此需要更全面、结构化的评测基准。
AI.method: 从近100个经典定理构建定理证明基准，并设计两种形式：plain版本（每个实例一个目标定理）和premised版本（将定理扩展为包含主要定理及自动提取的支撑子定理的结构化系列任务）。引入定理级覆盖率和token效率指标进行评估。
AI.result: 显式前提显著提升了Lean4证明器性能；当前证明器偏向于解决简单子定理，且常依赖冗长低效的tactic序列而非紧凑的证明计划。
AI.conclusion: TheoremBench提供了对形式化推理能力更细粒度的评估视角，强调了结构化基准设计对于评估Lean4定理证明器的重要性。

title: Precision Is Not Faithfulness: Coverage-Aware Evaluation of Grounded Generation with a Complete Oracle
comment: 8 pages, multilingual (EN/ES/PT). A reference-free faithfulness metric adding recall (coverage) against a complete structured oracle: precision-only rewards abstention; requiring coverage reorders models. Code: https://github.com/vectrayx/precision-is-not-faithfulness Demo: https://huggingface.co/spaces/jsantillana/faithful-strategy-engineer-f1
summary: Reference-free faithfulness metrics verify each atomic claim a model makes against ground truth, and are increasingly used to evaluate grounded generation. We show they share a blind spot: they measure only precision -- are the stated claims supported? -- and therefore reward abstention, since a model can score near-perfect faithfulness by saying almost nothing. We make this measurable using Formula 1 telemetry, a domain where strategic ground truth is derived deterministically and, crucially, completely: for each decision we know the full set of facts that mattered. This completeness -- absent in open-domain faithfulness benchmarks -- lets us measure recall (coverage of the relevant facts) exactly, alongside precision. On a multilingual (EN/ES/PT) benchmark of 7,253 decision instances spanning 150 races, the most precise frontier model covers under half of the relevant facts and ranks last by F1, so requiring coverage reorders the systems; the same effect reappears in a second complete-oracle domain (NOAA weather forecasts). A prompt ablation shows the low coverage is not an under-prompting artifact: explicitly asking models to be thorough does not close the gap. We pair faithfulness with coverage into a single score, validate the metric (controlled perturbation; agreement across a model-free regex extractor and a cross-family LLM extractor, system-level Spearman 1.0), and give a verifier-guided generation method that improves precision and recall without references. We release the benchmark, structured annotations, metric, baselines, and an interactive demo.

AI.tldr: 本文发现现有忠实度指标只衡量精确率（查准率）而忽略了召回率（查全率），导致模型说很少话也能得高分。作者利用F1赛车遥测数据（可获取完整事实）构建了同时衡量精确率和召回率的评测框架，发现最精确的模型覆盖不到一半相关事实，按F1得分排名最差。该现象在天气预测领域也得到验证。
AI.motivation: 现有忠实度指标存在盲点：只衡量精确率（模型所述 claims 是否有依据），忽略了召回率（模型是否覆盖了所有相关事实），导致模型通过"少说话"即可获得高忠实度分数。需要同时考量精确率和召回率的评估方法。
AI.method: 利用F1赛车遥测数据（可确定性推导出完整的事实集合）构建多语言（EN/ES/PT）基准，包含7,253个决策实例和150场比赛。同时评估精确率（claims是否被支持）和召回率（是否覆盖所有相关事实）。在NOAA天气预报领域进行验证。提出将忠实度与覆盖率合并为单一分数的指标，并用控制扰动实验和跨模型提取器验证有效性。给出无需参考的验证器引导生成方法。
AI.result: 最精确的模型覆盖不到一半相关事实，按F1得分排名最差。低覆盖率不是提示词不足导致的伪影——明确要求模型详尽也无济于事。在NOAA天气预报领域复现了相同效应。跨模型提取器系统级Spearman相关系数达到1.0。
AI.conclusion: 现有忠实度评估需要纳入召回率（覆盖率）维度，仅靠精确率会误导评估结果。作者发布了基准、结构化标注、指标、基线方法和交互式演示。

title: Pre-Intervention Prediction of Sparse Autoencoder Steering Side Effects
summary: Sparse autoencoder (SAE) features are increasingly used to steer language models, but feature steering is rarely clean: the same intervention can behave inconsistently across contexts and perturb unrelated features. We introduce a pre-intervention screening framework for forecasting SAE steering side effects from feature statistics computed before steering. We operationalize side effects along two axes of steering modularity, effect stability and collateral spread, and evaluate GPT-2-small, Pythia-70M-deduped, Gemma-2-2B, and Llama-3.1-8B across ReLU, JumpReLU, and TopK SAE dictionaries. Across these settings, decoder geometry, activation statistics, co-activation structure, and direct-logit footprint predict steering modularity better than frequency-only and activation-magnitude baselines. The signal is strongest in GPT-2-small, Pythia-70M, and Llama-3.1-8B, where it survives residualization against magnitude-related confounds, and weaker in Gemma-2-2B. Held-out screening shows that ranking unseen features by predicted cleanliness can select features that steer more cleanly on fresh contexts, but the successful axis varies by setting: GPT-2 improves most cleanly, Pythia improves mainly on stability, Llama mainly on collateral, and Gemma only partially. A controlled Llama Scope width comparison shows that the predictive signal persists under a 32K-to-128K dictionary-width change, although the screening payoff becomes less stable. Overall, SAE steering side effects are predictable in advance, but the useful predictor signature and transferred modularity axis are model- and dictionary-setting dependent.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种预干预筛选框架，通过SAE特征统计量预测特征引导的副作用（效果不稳定性和附带散播），在多种模型和字典设置下验证了该方法的有效性，但发现预测效果因模型和字典而异。
AI.motivation: SAE特征引导存在不一致性：同一干预在不同上下文中行为不同，且会扰动无关特征。现有方法缺乏对副作用的预测能力，因此需要一种预干预筛选框架来提前评估特征引导的模块化程度。
AI.method: 提出预干预筛选框架，从效果稳定性（effect stability）和附带散播（collateral spread）两个维度衡量模块化。使用解码器几何、激活统计、共激活结构和直接logit足迹作为预测因子，在GPT-2-small、Pythia-70M-deduped、Gemma-2-2B和Llama-3.1-8B上评估ReLU、JumpReLU和TopK SAE字典。
AI.result: 解码器几何等预测因子优于频率和激活幅度基线；GPT-2-small、Pythia-70M和Llama-3.1-8B中效果显著且能抵抗幅度混淆；Gemma-2-2B效果较弱。留出筛选可选出更干净的特征，但不同模型提升维度不同（GPT-2整体提升，Pythia侧重稳定性，Llama侧重附带散播，Gemma仅部分有效）。字典宽度变化时预测信号依然存在但筛选收益不稳定。
AI.conclusion: SAE特征引导的副作用是可提前预测的，但有效的预测因子和可传递的模块化维度依赖于具体模型和字典设置，不存在通用方案。

title: Emergent alignment and the projectability of ethical personas
summary: Work on `emergent misalignment' shows that finetuning LLMs on narrow tasks can induce broadly misaligned behavior. This supports the `persona selection' (PSM) hypothesis: during pre-training, LLMs learn to simulate different characters and perspectives, which can be elicited and refined during post-training. This paper investigates the converse phenomenon, `emergent alignment', and uses it to support and refine the PSM and motivate a novel desideratum for alignment. We finetune a helpful-only model on broad and narrow safety tasks. To create SFT samples, we follow the `Constitutional AI' (CAI) approach and use four constitutions which encode reasonable alignment strategies: deontology, consequentialism, virtue ethics, and aligning AIs as subordinate to human authority. For each of those models, we show that finetuning on two narrow safety sub-categories reliably induces emergent alignment over a representative set of general safety categories, and on safety subcategories that we directly filtered-out of the data sets used for narrow alignment. To test the `PSM' using a more fine-grained evaluation, we used a multidimensional `ethical persona' diagnostic. For each constitutionally finetuned (broad/narrow) model, we evaluate how well their behavior matches their expected signature profile. Our results show that our CAI models acquire their expected ``ethical persona'' -- e.g., the model narrowly fine-tuned on SFT samples created using the consequentialist constitution agrees significantly more with utilitarian than deontological beliefs. Yet our coarse and fine-grained evaluations show that there are significant differences across our (broad/narrow) finetuned CAI models in how well they project. We conclude that alignment strategies should be evaluated, not just on their (in-distribution) general safety performance, but also specifically on their degree of projectability.

AI.tldr: 本文研究了"新兴对齐"现象，通过对仅帮助型模型进行安全任务微调，发现窄范围安全训练能引发广泛的安全对齐行为。研究还通过"伦理人格"诊断验证了"人格选择假说"，并提出对齐策略应评估其"可投射性"。
AI.motivation: 已有工作发现"新兴错位"现象（窄任务微调导致广泛错位行为），支持"人格选择假说"（PSM）。本文研究相反现象"新兴对齐"，以进一步支持和完善PSM，并提出新的对齐评估维度。
AI.method: 1. 基于四种不同伦理准则（道义论、后果论、德性伦理、AI服从人类权威）构建CAI数据。2. 对仅帮助型模型进行宽范围和窄范围安全任务微调。3. 使用多维"伦理人格"诊断进行细粒度评估，检验模型行为是否符合预期伦理特征。
AI.result: 窄范围安全微调可靠地引发了对广泛安全类别的新兴对齐。CAI模型获得了预期"伦理人格"，例如后果论微调模型在功利主义信念上显著高于道义论信念。但宽/窄微调模型在"可投射性"上存在显著差异。
AI.conclusion: 对齐策略不仅应评估其分布内的一般安全性能，还应专门评估其"可投射性"（即模型将训练时学到的对齐行为泛化到新场景的能力）。

title: LexRubric: A Rubric-Guided Diagnostic Benchmark for Open-Ended Legal Tasks
summary: As large language models (LLMs) are increasingly applied to real-world legal tasks, evaluating the reliability of their open-ended legal responses has become essential. These tasks require context-sensitive answers and allow little room for error, motivating fine-grained and diagnostic evaluation that can identify specific sources of response quality failures. We introduce LexRubric, a rubric-based benchmark for evaluating open-ended Chinese legal tasks. LexRubric contains 649 instances from legal consultation and judicial examination, which reflect both everyday legal needs and professional legal reasoning and cover 14 legal scenarios. It further includes 12,337 expert-written atomic scoring criteria organized under a unified six-dimensional framework, enabling accurate evaluation and diagnostic analysis across tasks and evaluation dimensions. To validate the reliability of the evaluation, we test multiple judge models and compare model-based judgments with human judgments. We further evaluate 18 recent general and legal-domain LLMs on LexRubric. Results show that different models exhibit distinct capability profiles, and that open-ended legal question remains challenging for current LLMs. Data is available at: https://github.com/foggpoy/LexRubric.

AI.tldr: LexRubric是一个基于评分细则的基准，用于评估开放型中文法律任务，包含649个实例和12,337条专家编写的原子评分标准，覆盖14个法律场景和六维评估框架。测试18个LLM后发现开放型法律问答对当前模型仍具挑战性。
AI.motivation: LLM在法律任务中的实际应用日益增多，但现有评估缺乏对开放型法律回答的细粒度、诊断性评价，难以定位回答质量失败的具体原因。
AI.method: 构建LexRubric基准：收集649个来自法律咨询和司法考试的实例，覆盖14个法律场景；由专家编写12,337条原子评分标准，统一在六维评估框架下；使用多个评判模型并与人类判断对比验证可靠性。
AI.result: 不同模型展现出不同的能力轮廓；当前LLM在开放型法律问答任务上仍然面临挑战。
AI.conclusion: 开放型法律问答对现有LLM仍具挑战性，LexRubric可作为细粒度诊断评估的有效工具。

title: An Information-Theoretic Definition for Open-Ended Learning
summary: A growing body of work points to the great promise of AI systems that can continually expand their capabilities as they operate in an open-ended environment. But yet there is no coherent definition of open-endedness or theory about how an agent ought to explore an open-ended environment. We introduce an information-theoretic definition based on a new concept -- the ${\textit bit-equivalent}$ -- which quantifies the information required to attain each level of expected reward. We consider an environment to be open-ended if an agent can attain linear growth in the bit-equivalent. We establish that classical bandit environments are not open-ended and formulate a bandit environment that is. We also introduce an algorithm that achieves open-ended learning in this environment.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了基于"比特等价"的信息论定义来量化开放式环境，并证明了经典赌博机环境不具备开放性，同时设计了可实现开放学习的算法。
AI.motivation: 尽管AI系统在开放式环境中持续扩展能力展现出巨大潜力，但目前缺乏对开放性的统一定义以及智能体应如何探索开放式环境的理论指导。
AI.method: 提出了"比特等价"（bit-equivalent）这一新概念，用信息论量化达到每个预期奖励水平所需的信息量；将环境开放定义为智能体能够实现比特等价的线性增长；并设计了相应的算法。
AI.result: 证明了经典赌博机环境不具备开放性，同时构造了一个满足开放性定义的赌博机环境，并验证了所提算法能在此环境中实现开放学习。
AI.conclusion: 为开放性问题提供了首个信息论定义和理论框架，为后续开放式学习研究奠定了理论基础。

title: LLM-Orchestrated Conformance Checking in Stroke Care Without Computer-Interpretable Guidelines
summary:
Objective: Conformance checking in healthcare seeks to assess whether patient care pathways adhere to clinical guidelines. However, its practical application often depends on the availability of formal, machine-interpretable representations of guidelines, such as Computer-Interpretable Guidelines (CIGs), which are seldom available in real-world clinical settings.
  Methods: This work introduces a modular framework based on the orchestration of Large Language Models (LLMs) to support medical conformance checking directly from unstructured clinical and guideline texts, without requiring predefined CIGs. The proposed architecture integrates multiple LLMs and supporting components to extract patient traces from clinical discharge letters, identify normative rules from textual clinical guidelines, translate these rules into executable scripts, and compute a Trace Conformance Indicator to quantify compliance within the event log.
  Results: The framework was implemented and evaluated in the stroke care domain at the neurological ward of Alessandria Hospital. Hundreds of patient traces were automatically extracted from hospital data and assessed against 50 rules derived from the reference guideline. The analysis showed that more than 86\% of the available traces were conformant.
  Conclusion: The results demonstrate the feasibility of using orchestrated LLMs for practical healthcare conformance analysis. At the same time, the study provides evidence of a high level of adherence to stroke care guidelines at Alessandria Hospital.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一个基于大语言模型编排的模块化框架，无需预定义计算机可解释指南，即可从非结构化临床文本和指南中直接进行医疗合规性检查。在卒中护理领域验证，超过86%的患者轨迹符合指南规则。
AI.motivation: 医疗合规性检查的实际应用通常依赖于计算机可解释指南（CIGs）这类形式化、机器可读的指南表示，但在真实临床环境中很少具备这些条件，限制了合规性检查的广泛应用。
AI.method: 提出一个基于LLM编排的模块化框架，包含多个LLM和支撑组件：从临床出院信中提取患者轨迹、从文本指南中识别规范性规则、将规则转化为可执行脚本，并计算轨迹合规性指标来量化事件日志中的合规程度。
AI.result: 在亚历山德里亚医院神经内科病房的卒中护理领域实施评估，自动提取了数百条患者轨迹，依据参考指南推导出的50条规则进行评估，超过86%的轨迹是合规的。
AI.conclusion: 结果表明，使用编排的LLM进行实际医疗合规性分析是可行的，同时验证了该医院卒中护理指南具有高水平的依从性。

title: PriFT: Prior-Support Guided Supervised Fine-Tuning
comment: The first two authors contributed equally to this work
summary: Supervised fine-tuning (SFT) is an efficient approach for downstream task adaptation and often serves as the initialization stage for reinforcement learning (RL), but it can show weaker generalization than RL. A key limitation is its off-policy objective: SFT fits fixed demonstrations token by token, including targets poorly aligned with the model's pretrained distribution, which can lead to overfitting. A recent line of work addresses this issue by assigning larger training weights to tokens better aligned with the current model's predictive distribution, with the intuition that fitting these tokens are less distortive to the model's pretrained knowledge and representations. However, computing the token weights from the model that is currently fine-tuned entangles token weights with the optimization trajectory, inducing a self-reinforcing dynamics as the distribution rapidly departs from the pretrained model. To address this, we propose PriFT (Prior-support guided Fine-Tuning), which derives token weights from a frozen pretrained reference to obtain a stable reweighting signal unaffected by fine-tuning. This signal estimates prior support: the extent to which each target token is supported by the pretrained distribution. Across multiple existing token-reweighting rules, replacing the reweighting signal from the online model to pretrained model consistently improves performance. We introduce two instantiations: PriFT-prob uses pretrained token probability, while PriFT-mass selects tokens by cumulative probability mass under the pretrained distribution. Extensive experiments on mathematical reasoning, code generation, and medical question answering show that PriFT achieves state-of-the-art results among SFT baselines and provides a better initialization for subsequent RL training.

AI.tldr: 针对SFT因对偶拟合固定演示导致泛化差的问题，提出PriFT方法，用冻结的预训练模型计算token权重（先验支持度）替代在线模型权重，避免自我强化的动态偏差，在数学推理、代码生成、医疗问答上取得SOTA并改善后续RL初始化。
AI.motivation: SFT采用off-policy目标逐token拟合固定演示，部分token与模型预训练分布不一致，导致过拟合和泛化弱于RL。现有方法用当前微调模型计算token权重存在自我强化动态（分布快速偏离预训练模型），需要稳定的权重信号。
AI.method: 提出PriFT方法，使用冻结的预训练参考模型计算token权重（先验支持度），包括两种实例化：PriFT-prob（预训练token概率）和PriFT-mass（累积概率质量选择）。将在线模型的权重信号替换为预训练模型的稳定信号。
AI.result: 在数学推理、代码生成、医疗问答任务上，PriFT在SFT基线中达到SOTA，且为后续RL训练提供更好的初始化。
AI.conclusion: 用预训练模型的稳定先验支持度替代在线模型的动态token权重，能有效缓解SFT的过拟合和泛化问题，提升下游任务性能及RL初始化质量。

title: Few-step Cofolding with All-Atom Flow Maps
summary: All-atom generative modeling of 3D biomolecular complexes has emerged as the dominant paradigm for predicting the structure of proteins and protein-ligand systems. Generating structures at the atomic level of fidelity, however, typically requires expensive iterative diffusion rollouts, making both conventional deployment and inference-time search techniques computationally costly. In this paper, we introduce the Denoiser Cofolding All-Atom Flowmap (DeCAF) framework for distilling state-of-the-art all-atom cofolding models into all-atom flow maps that produce high-quality samples in only a few inference steps. We build DeCAF on a denoiser-based formulation of flow maps with endpoint losses that naturally support SE(3) rigid alignment, which we show is critical for training accurate models. We further derive a simple change of variables that lets DeCAF operate in the σ-space noise schedule of EDM-style architectures, enabling direct distillation from pretrained cofolding diffusion models. Equipped with DeCAF's flowmap lookahead, we introduce a purpose-built inference-time framework that improves sampling through reward-guided search. Empirically, DeCAF-Boltz statistically improves over Boltz-1x in both accuracy (RMSD) and physical validity scores of protein-ligand poses at strict NFE budgets on the challenging Runs N' Poses, while also showing a more optimal Pareto frontier across all inference compute budgets on PoseBusters. Distilling the state-of-the-art Pearl cofolding model, DeCAF-Pearl outperforms diffusion-based cofolding models and matches its teacher on success rate while using 5x fewer NFEs. We release our code at https://github.com/genesistherapeutics/decaf.

AI.tldr: DeCAF框架通过将最先进的全原子共折叠扩散模型蒸馏为流映射（flow maps），仅需少量推理步骤即可生成高质量3D生物分子复合物结构，并引入推理时奖励引导搜索进一步提升采样质量，在多项基准测试中超越原扩散模型，同时将推理步数减少5倍。
AI.motivation: 现有全原子生成模型通常需要昂贵的迭代扩散 rollout，导致部署和推理时搜索的计算成本过高，亟需一种高效且保持高质量的方法。
AI.method: 基于去噪器的流映射公式，引入端点损失以支持SE(3)刚性对齐；通过简单变量变换适配EDM架构的σ-space噪声调度，实现从预训练共折叠扩散模型的直接蒸馏；并设计推理时奖励引导搜索框架。
AI.result: DeCAF-Boltz在Runs N' Poses上统计上优于Boltz-1x（RMSD和物理有效性）；DeCAF-Pearl在成功率上匹配教师模型，同时使用5倍更少的NFE；在PoseBusters上展示更优的Pareto前沿。
AI.conclusion: DeCAF框架有效解决了全原子扩散模型推理成本高的问题，通过蒸馏和推理时搜索实现了高效且高质量的结构生成。

title: Deterministic Integrity Gates for LLM-Assisted Clinical Manuscript Preparation: An Auditable Biomedical Informatics Architecture
comment: 28 pages, 3 figures, 4 tables; includes supplementary material (deterministic-detector inventory, per-class defect breakdown, worked example). Software (MIT): https://github.com/Aperivue/medsci-skills ; archived on Zenodo (concept DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20155321; v3.8.0 version DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20582972)
summary: Objective. Large language models (LLMs) increasingly draft clinical research manuscripts, but their fluency can hide fabricated citations, numbers that drift from source tables, and unmet reporting-guideline items. Existing tools generate text without verifying it, and self-critique inherits the blind spots that produce confident fabrication. We describe an architecture that pairs generation with verification. Methods. The design rests on three principles: decompose the workflow into self-contained skills, gate every stage transition with halt-on-failure, and resolve each integrity question with the cheapest sufficient mechanism -- a deterministic, re-executable check where one suffices, and a prose-level probe only where interpretation is unavoidable. This determinism-where-possible split, organized as an integrity-gate taxonomy, is the core contribution. It is realized as MedSci Skills, an open-source toolkit of 43 skills coordinated by one orchestrator, whose deterministic tier comprises 21 standard-library detectors. We evaluate it on three reproducible public-dataset pipelines (STARD, PRISMA, STROBE) and a seeded-defect ablation. Results. Across the three pipelines every content-hash manifest verified clean and the gates surfaced real defects. On 27 identical injected defects the deterministic gates detected all 27 with no false positives on the matched clean fixtures, whereas a generic single-prompt LLM reviewer detected 11, its misses concentrated in generated-code, bibliography-internal, and style defects the prose does not expose. Conclusion. Determinism-where-possible verification yields an auditable, re-executable trail that exposes the evidence a human needs to check an LLM-assisted manuscript -- feasibility and reproducibility evidence, not a claim of human-competitive quality, which a separate blinded study addresses. MedSci Skills is MIT-licensed and archived (v3.8.0).

AI.tldr: 本文提出MedSci Skills架构，通过"尽可能确定性验证"原则（可确定性检查用可重执行检测器，不可确定性才用LLM探针），结合43个技能模块和31个确定性检测器，有效检测LLM生成临床研究稿件中的虚假引用、数据漂移和报告规范缺失等问题，在三个公开数据集上实现100%缺陷检出率且零误报。
AI.motivation: 现有LLM生成临床研究稿件时，流畅性掩盖了虚假引用、数据偏移和未满足的报告规范等问题。现有工具只生成不验证，自我批评机制继承了产生自信造假的盲点。因此需要一种将生成与验证配对的新架构。
AI.method: 基于三条设计原则：将工作流分解为独立技能、每个阶段转换设置halt-on-failure门控、用最小充分机制解决完整性验证问题——确定性可重执行检查优先，仅当需要解释时使用散文级探针。实现为MedSci Skills开源工具包，含43个技能模块和31个确定性检测器，通过一个编排器协调。
AI.result: 在STARD、PRISMA、STROBE三个公开数据集上，所有内容哈希清单验证干净，门控机制暴露真实缺陷。在27个注入缺陷的消融实验中，确定性门控检测全部27个且零误报，而通用单提示LLM审查器仅检测到11个。
AI.conclusion: "尽可能确定性验证"方法生成可审计、可重执行的验证轨迹，为人类检查LLM辅助稿件提供所需证据。该方法提供的是可行性和可复现性证据，而非声称达到人类竞争质量。

title: Introducing multiplex semantic networks as multifaceted representations of creative associative knowledge across multilingual samples
summary: Creativity is a complex cognitive ability that relies on knowledge organisation and retrieval from semantic memory. Yet most research uses a single task to measure it, capturing only a fraction of this complexity. This study investigates multiplex networks - layered semantic networks obtained from six cognitive tasks - as a more comprehensive approach to modelling the associative knowledge underlying creativity. We collected data from N=518 individuals from four countries (Austria, USA, Singapore, Italy). From their responses to verbal fluency, sentence-chain, free association, and narrative writing tasks, we constructed semantic networks and assembled them in a multiplex structure. AI persona-based responses provided a comparison baseline. Structural reducibility analyses showed that different task layers captured distinct, non-redundant information about semantic organisation, supporting the use of multiple tasks over any single one. The networks from high- and low-creative groups remained structurally distinct, while AI-generated networks showed near-identical structures regardless of creativity group. Finally, we used 12 features (network measures, emotional scores, and spreading activation simulations) in a machine learning model using ridge regression to predict individual creativity scores. The combination of structurally similar layers, as identified in the previous stage, improved a proof-of-concept prediction accuracy by 50%. Structural measures showed the highest feature importance, with spreading activation dynamics providing additional predictive power. Together, these findings indicate that multiplex semantic networks capture a richer, cross-cultural picture of associative knowledge underlying creativity. We also release our diverse dataset and code to foster diverse computational approaches within the creativity community.

AI.tldr: 本文提出使用多重语义网络（multiplex networks）——从六种认知任务中构建的分层语义网络——来更全面地建模创造力背后的联想知识。研究收集了来自四个国家518名参与者的数据，通过结构可约性分析、高低创造力组对比、AI基线比较以及机器学习预测，证明了多任务方法优于单一任务，并将预测准确率提升了50%。
AI.motivation: 传统创造力研究大多依赖单一认知任务来衡量创造力，只能捕捉这一复杂认知能力的部分维度。作者希望探索一种更全面的方法来建模创造力背后的联想知识结构。
AI.method: 从语词流畅性、句子链、自由联想和叙事写作等六种任务中为518名来自四个国家的参与者构建语义网络，组成多重网络结构。使用结构可约性分析、高低创造力组对比、AI基线比较，以及基于12种特征（网络指标、情绪分数、传播激活模拟）的岭回归机器学习模型进行预测。
AI.result: 不同任务层捕捉了语义组织的不同非冗余信息；高低创造力组的多重网络结构存在显著差异，而AI生成网络在不同创造力组间结构几乎相同；结合结构相似层可将创造力预测准确率提升50%，其中网络结构指标特征重要性最高，传播激活动态提供额外预测力。
AI.conclusion: 多重语义网络能够更丰富地捕捉创造力背后的联想知识结构，具有跨文化适用性。多任务方法优于单一任务方法，为创造力研究提供了更全面的计算建模框架。

title: RiskNet: A large-scale dataset of AI risk incidents from news with alignment and multi-dimensional annotations
comment: The manuscript has been submitted to Scientific Data
summary: As artificial intelligence (AI) systems are increasingly deployed across socially consequential domains, reports of AI-related harms and failures have grown in frequency and diversity. Although existing governance frameworks articulate high-level principles for responsible AI, large-scale empirical resources for tracking and analyzing real-world AI risk incidents remain limited. Existing incident collections are often manually curated, relatively small in scale, and insufficient for continuous, data-driven monitoring and downstream computational analysis. To address this need, we present RiskNet, a large-scale dataset of AI risk incidents constructed from large-scale multilingual news sources. RiskNet applies a structured pipeline for AI risk news identification, event-level report screening, incident alignment, and multi-dimensional incident classification. The resulting resource organizes dispersed news reports into incident-centered records and provides benchmark datasets for event classification, incident alignment, and incident-level risk labeling. In its current release, RiskNet covers hundreds of millions of source records and yields a large-scale collection of AI risk-related reports, including aligned incident clusters and annotated benchmark subsets. The dataset is also accessible through an online platform for browsing and exploration. We describe the data sources, processing workflow, taxonomy design, and technical validation of the resource. RiskNet is intended to support downstream research on AI safety, governance, risk analysis, and benchmarking, as well as longitudinal and cross-source analyses of AI-related harms. By providing a structured and reusable empirical resource, RiskNet helps bridge the gap between high-level governance principles and the documented realities of AI risk incidents.

AI.tldr: RiskNet是一个从多语言新闻源构建的大规模AI风险事件数据集，通过结构化流程实现事件识别、报告筛选、事件对齐和多维分类，旨在弥补AI治理原则与真实风险事件之间的鸿沟，支持AI安全、治理和风险分析等下游研究。
AI.motivation: 现有AI风险事件数据集多为人工整理、规模较小，难以支撑大规模数据驱动的持续监控和计算分析，亟需一个大规模、结构化的实证资源来连接高层治理原则与实际AI风险事件。
AI.method: 从大规模多语言新闻源出发，设计结构化流水线，包括AI风险新闻识别、事件级报告筛选、事件对齐以及多维事件分类，将分散的新闻报道整理为以事件为中心的记录，并提供事件分类、对齐和风险标注的基准数据集。
AI.result: RiskNet覆盖数亿条源记录，产出大规模AI风险相关报告集合，包含对齐的事件聚类和带标注的基准子集，并通过在线平台提供浏览和探索功能。
AI.conclusion: RiskNet为AI安全、治理、风险分析和基准测试等下游研究提供了结构化的可复用实证资源，支持纵向和跨来源的AI危害分析，有效弥合了高层治理原则与现实AI风险事件之间的差距。

title: From Rigid to Dynamic: Entropy-Guided Adaptive Inference for Long-Context LLMs
summary: Existing sparse attention and KV cache compression methods for long-context LLM inference typically apply fixed sparsity patterns or uniform budgets across all attention heads, overlooking the substantial variation in attention behavior among heads and contexts. We observe two distinct entropy patterns among attention heads: Rigid Heads, whose entropy stays near zero across input segments, and Dynamic Heads, whose entropy fluctuates significantly. Crucially, the distribution of these types is context-dependent and cannot be predetermined offline. We therefore propose EntropyInfer, a training-free framework that uses attention entropy to adaptively allocate compute at the granularity of individual heads and segments during prefilling. For decoding, we introduce a latent KV cache compression scheme that leverages generated output tokens, rather than prefill tokens alone, to identify and retain the most critical cache entries. Extensive experiments on Llama, Qwen and openPangu model series show that EntropyInfer consistently outperforms baselines including SnapKV, AdaKV, and CritiPrefill, achieving up to 2.39$\times$ end-to-end speedup beyond 100k tokens with minimal quality degradation compared to full attention. The code is released in https://github.com/SHA-4096/EntropyInfer.

AI.tldr: 提出EntropyInfer框架，利用注意力熵动态分配计算资源（预填充阶段按头/段粒度）并设计潜在KV缓存压缩方案（利用生成token而非仅预填充token），在长上下文LLM推理中实现最高2.39倍加速。
AI.motivation: 现有稀疏注意力和KV缓存压缩方法对所有注意力头采用固定稀疏模式或统一预算，忽视了不同头与上下文间注意力行为的巨大差异。
AI.method: 1) 识别两类注意力头：Rigid Heads（熵近零）和Dynamic Heads（熵波动大）；2) 预填充阶段：基于注意力熵自适应分配计算资源（按头+段粒度）；3) 解码阶段：利用生成token（而非仅预填充token）进行潜在KV缓存压缩，保留最关键的缓存条目。
AI.result: 在Llama、Qwen和openPangu系列模型上，EntropyInfer在超过100k token时实现最高2.39倍端到端加速，性能优于SnapKV、AdaKV、CritiPrefill等基线方法，且质量损失极小。
AI.conclusion: 注意力头的rigid/dynamic分布是上下文依赖的，无法离线预判；基于熵的自适应分配和利用生成token的KV压缩是有效的长上下文推理优化策略。

title: What Should a Skill Remember? Quality-Cost Trade-offs in Cost-Aware Skill Rewriting for Language Model Agents
summary: Large language model agents increasingly rely on skills: reusable procedural documents encoding workflows, tool use, implementation patterns, validation checks, and domain rules. Skill rewriting is often treated as prompt compression, but shorter skills can make agents more expensive by removing sparse operational anchors that prevent exploration, debugging, and recovery. We study skill rewriting through this economic lens. Our controlled framework profiles skill structure, rewrites skills using information-preservation strategies, and evaluates the rewrites under fixed task instructions, environments, and verifiers. Experiments on SkillsBench reveal distinct quality--cost trade-offs across strategies: API/code anchoring, workflow guarding, and rule/formula anchoring benefit different task families, with no universally dominant template. In the main held-out evaluation, the learned policy reduces total cost by 7.0\% and downstream agent-token cost by 6.0\%; in frozen cross-model transfer, the corresponding reductions average 14.7\% and 13.7\%, while verifier quality is preserved. These results position skill design as cost-aware operational knowledge engineering rather than prompt compression. Resources: \href{https://github.com/1Reminding/Skill_EE}{SkillEE}.

AI.tldr: 本文提出技能重写不应仅视为提示压缩，而应作为成本感知的操作知识工程。通过控制框架分析技能结构、采用信息保留策略重写技能，并在固定任务指令、环境和验证器下评估。实验显示不同策略在不同任务族中存在质量-成本权衡，学习策略在保留验证质量的同时降低了总成本和下游代理令牌成本。
AI.motivation: 现有研究常将技能重写视为提示压缩问题，追求更短的技能描述。但作者指出，缩短技能可能因移除稀疏操作锚点（如探索、调试、恢复的关键信息）反而使代理更昂贵，因此需要从经济视角重新审视技能重写问题。
AI.method: 构建了一个受控框架：1）剖析技能结构（profile skill structure）；2）采用信息保留策略（如API/代码锚定、工作流保护、规则/公式锚定）重写技能；3）在固定任务指令、环境和验证器下评估重写效果。在SkillsBench上进行实验，并学习最优策略。
AI.result: 1）不同策略在不同任务族中存在质量-成本权衡，无通用最优模板；2）学习策略在保留验证质量前提下，总成本降低7.0%，下游代理令牌成本降低6.0%；3）跨模型冻结迁移中，成本降低平均达14.7%和13.7%。
AI.conclusion: 技能设计应定位为成本感知的操作知识工程，而非简单的提示压缩问题。不同任务需匹配不同的信息保留策略，学习到的策略具有良好的跨模型泛化能力。

title: STAR-KV: Low-Rank KV Cache Compression via Soft Thresholding for Adaptive Rank Control
summary: Low-rank projection has emerged as a promising approach for compressing the KV cache by exploiting hidden-dimension redundancy. However, prior methods rely on fixed or heuristic rank selection and struggle to achieve aggressive compression with minimal accuracy degradation. We propose STAR-KV, an adaptive low-rank KV cache compression framework with fine-grained rank control. STAR-KV encompasses 1) a differentiable thresholding mechanism that enables optimal rank selection at both attention-head and block levels, 2) a hybrid decomposition strategy that applies different low-rank factorizations according to the sensitivity of key and value projections, and 3) a low-rank-aware mixed precision quantization that leverages data statistics for near lossless low-bit quantization. Evaluated across multiple LLMs and benchmarks, STAR-KV achieves up to 75% KV cache compression and up to 20x overall KV cache reduction when combined with quantization. Enabled by custom Triton-based GPU kernels, STAR-KV delivers up to 6.9x speedup for the attention module and 3.1x end-to-end generation throughput. Our code is publicly available at: https://github.com/PriyanshBhatnagar/STAR-KV.

AI.tldr: STAR-KV 是一种自适应低秩 KV 缓存压缩框架，通过细粒度秩选择、混合分解和低秩量化，在多种 LLM 上实现高达 75% 压缩率，结合量化可达 20 倍缩减，并带来最高 6.9 倍注意力模块加速和 3.1 倍端到端吞吐提升。
AI.motivation: 现有低秩 KV 缓存压缩方法依赖固定或启发式秩选择，难以在激进压缩下保持精度，亟需一种自适应、细粒度的秩控制方法。
AI.method: 1) 可微分阈值机制实现注意力头级和块级的最优秩选择；2) 混合分解策略根据键/值投影的敏感性应用不同的低秩分解；3) 低秩感知混合精度量化，利用数据统计实现近乎无损的低比特量化。
AI.result: 在多个 LLM 和基准上，KV 缓存压缩率达 75%，结合量化后总压缩达 20 倍；注意力模块加速 6.9 倍，端到端生成吞吐提升 3.1 倍。
AI.conclusion: STAR-KV 通过自适应、细粒度的低秩压缩框架，显著优于固定秩方法，在压缩率、精度和推理速度上均实现突破，代码已开源。

title: AI Scientists Are Only as Good as Their Evidence: A Stratified Ablation of Proprietary Data and Reasoning Skills in Drug-Asset Valuation
summary: AI Scientist agents are often evaluated as if capability were mainly a function of model quality, prompting, or reasoning scaffolds. We test a different hypothesis in drug-asset valuation: for knowledge-intensive scientific decisions, the limiting factor is often the evidence substrate the agent can access. We run a controlled three-arm ablation on a production valuation agent: A is a plain web-only LLM analyst, B adds public structured tools plus a 14-dimension valuation playbook, verifier, objectivity policy and red-team, and C adds the proprietary Noah AI corpus of curated pipeline, trial and deal intelligence. Across a 13-asset stratified benchmark, B improves calibration and audit discipline: tier-in-range accuracy rises from 0.80 to 0.89 and objectivity from 3.16 to 3.30. But B does not remove the factual ceiling. Under capability-superset accounting, A and B recover only 0.25 and 0.38 of the curated gold competitive record, while C recovers 0.96; on the curated long-tail subset, C reaches 0.93 vs. 0.26/0.30. Raw blind-panel decision quality is similar for A and B (7.01 vs. 6.96), so we introduce completeness-aware decision utility: informed decision-quality = decision-quality x gold-coverage. On this metric, C reaches 7.43 vs. 1.76/2.57 for A/B. Even a perfect non-proprietary-data report would be capped at 3.83 by B's coverage. The result is not that reasoning scaffolds are unimportant; they improve calibration and discipline. Rather, proprietary evidence sets the upper bound of what the AI Scientist can know and therefore decide.

AI.tldr: 在知识密集型科学决策中，AI科学家的能力上限主要取决于其可访问的证据基底（proprietary evidence），而非仅依赖模型质量或推理框架。通过对照实验证明，即使推理框架优化了校准和纪律性，但专有数据语料库（Noah AI）将决策覆盖率从0.38提升至0.96，决策效用从2.57提升至7.43，形成了无法绕开的事实天花板。
AI.motivation: 挑战当前AI科学家评估中“能力主要取决于模型质量、提示或推理框架”的主流假设，提出在知识密集型领域（如药物资产估值）中，证据基底的可用性才是决定性瓶颈。
AI.method: 在真实生产级估值Agent上进行三臂消融实验：(A)纯网络LLM分析师；(B)增加公开结构化工具+14维估值剧本、验证器、客观性策略与红队；(C)进一步增加专有Noah AI语料库（涵盖管线、试验和交易情报）。在13资产分层基准上评估校准、客观性、覆盖率和决策效用。
AI.result: (1) B提升了校准（tier-in-range准确率0.80→0.89）和客观性（3.16→3.30）；(2) 但B未突破事实上限：A和B仅恢复0.25/0.38的金标记录，C恢复0.96；长尾子集C达0.93 vs 0.26/0.30；(3) 原始决策质量A/B相近（7.01 vs 6.96），但引入覆盖率调整后，C达7.43 vs 1.76/2.57；(4) 即使完美的非专有数据报告也被B的覆盖率限制在3.83。
AI.conclusion: 推理框架并非不重要（它们改善校准和纪律），但专有证据决定了AI科学家“能知道什么”的上限，从而也决定了其决策质量的上限。数据主权是知识密集型AI科学家的核心能力瓶颈。

title: Toward Signing Activity Projection in Sign Language Interaction
summary: Social robots must interact robustly not only with users assumed by speech-centered systems but also with diverse users whose communication relies on different modalities, e.g., sign language. One important capability gap is predictive turn-taking with signing users. Although Voice Activity Projection (VAP) has been successfully used to model future voice activity in spoken interaction, it remains unclear whether the framework transfers to sign language interaction. This paper presents an initial transfer study of adapting a VAP architecture to dyadic sign language interaction. Using interaction recordings from the Public DGS Corpus, we derive binary signing activity streams from lexical sign annotations and formulate proxy tasks for turn-taking prediction. The model uses pose-derived hand, eye-region, and mouth-region features extracted for each signer. The results show that SHIFT/HOLD prediction is promising, especially with hand cues, while SHIFT-prediction remains difficult. These findings provide initial evidence for both the promise and the current limitations of transferring predictive turn-taking models from spoken interaction to sign language interaction. Predictive modeling of sign language interaction still requires sign-language-specific event definitions that go beyond speech-derived categories.

AI.tldr: 本文首次尝试将语音交互中的VAP（语音活动预测）框架迁移到手语交互中，使用公共DGS语料库的双人对话数据，利用手、眼、嘴的姿势特征预测签语的轮换切换（SHIFT/HOLD）。结果表明hand特征对HOLD预测效果较好，但SHIFT预测仍困难，说明手语轮换预测需要超越语音衍生类别的、手语特有的事件定义。
AI.motivation: 现有的语音活动预测（VAP）框架在语音交互中成功实现了轮换预测，但面对依赖手语等非语音模态的多样用户群体时，机器缺乏对应的预测能力。为实现包容性社交机器人，亟需研究VAP框架能否迁移到手语交互场景。
AI.method: 基于Public DGS Corpus双人互动录影，从词汇级签语标注中提取二值签语活动流，构建轮换预测的代理任务。使用基于姿态的手部、眼部区域、口部区域特征，对每个签语者分别提取，并训练模型预测SHIFT（轮换切换）和HOLD（保持当前说话状态）。
AI.result: SHIFT/HOLD预测在手部特征上表现较好，特别是HOLD预测较为可靠；但SHIFT预测整体难度较大，准确率有限。
AI.conclusion: 将VAP框架从语音交互迁移到手语交互既展示了可行性（HOLD预测），也暴露了局限性（SHIFT预测困难）。未来需要定义手语特有的事件类别，而非简单套用语音衍生的轮换概念。

title: PRISM: Recovering Instruction Sets from Language Model Activations
summary: As LLMs are deployed as agents, reliable monitoring requires knowing not only what they output, but which instructions are steering their behavior. This is difficult when models infer unintended subgoals, follow contextual cues, or are influenced by prompt injections and hidden objectives. While activation-to-language methods suggest that hidden states can reveal natural-language information, existing approaches are not designed to recover the full set of simultaneous instructions, constraints, prohibitions, and subgoals active in agentic settings. We formalize this problem as instruction set retrieval and introduce PRISM, an activation-conditioned interpreter that decodes hidden states from a frozen target model into a faithful bullet list of active instructions. Unlike prior activation-to-language methods, PRISM is trained to recover instruction sets directly, using judge-guided GRPO to reward covered instructions and penalize unsupported ones. Across benign, constrained, prompt-injection, and hidden-objective settings, PRISM outperforms activation-to-language baselines, especially on security-relevant objectives.

AI.tldr: 本文提出PRISM方法，通过激活条件解码器从LLM隐藏状态中恢复完整的活跃指令集（包括子目标、约束、禁止项等），在良性、约束、提示注入和隐藏目标设置下均优于现有方法。
AI.motivation: LLM作为代理部署时，仅监控输出内容不足以确保可靠性，需要知道哪些指令正在引导模型行为。现有激活到语言方法无法恢复代理场景中同时活跃的完整指令集（包括子目标、上下文线索、提示注入和隐藏目标）。
AI.method: 将问题形式化为指令集检索任务，提出PRISM——一个激活条件解释器，通过冻结的目标模型的隐藏状态解码为忠实的指令列表。使用基于评判的GRPO强化学习进行训练，奖励覆盖的指令并惩罚无依据的指令。
AI.result: 在良性、约束、提示注入和隐藏目标四种设置下，PRISM在指令集恢复任务上优于现有的激活到语言方法，特别是在安全相关目标的恢复上表现突出。
AI.conclusion: PRISM能够有效地从LLM隐藏状态中恢复完整的活跃指令集，为AI代理的可靠监控提供了新工具，尤其对安全相关场景具有重要意义。

title: Guide Me Out: A Framework to Benchmark VLM Operators Communication in Crisis Scenarios
summary: Effective crisis response requires spatially grounded communication that bridges linguistic guidance of civilians with the physical environment, accounting for structural bottlenecks, evolving threats, and agent-specific contexts. Yet, current NLP research in crisis communication remains mainly limited to static, text-only classification settings, overlooking the critical communicative role of AI operators in dynamic, embodied scenarios. We address this gap with a novel benchmarking framework for evaluating Vision-Language Models (VLMs) tasked with guiding civilian agents through simulated evacuations. We test two communication strategies (narrowcast vs. broadcast), two environment representations (visual vs. graph-based), and two threat behaviors (static vs. moving) across nine maps of varying structural complexity. Our results show that Narrowcast consistently reduces civilian Fail rates compared to Broadcast across all difficulty levels. Guidance quality depends heavily on how the VLM operator represents the world: the visual modality drives performance, while adding an adjacency graph is model-dependent and often harmful. Moving threats raise Fail rates across all conditions as communication must continuously adapt over time. Together, these findings show that deploying VLMs as AI operators in evacuation scenarios remains a non-trivial challenge, where the choice of communication strategy and input representation can directly determine the success or failure of the intervention.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一个评估视觉语言模型（VLMs）在模拟疏散场景中引导平民的新基准框架，发现窄播策略优于广播、视觉模态驱动性能、移动威胁增加失败率，表明VLMs作为AI操作员在动态应急沟通中仍面临重大挑战。
AI.motivation: 当前的NLP危机沟通研究局限于静态文本分类，忽略了AI操作员在动态、具身场景中的关键沟通角色，因此需要评估VLMs在复杂、动态的疏散引导任务中的表现。
AI.method: 构建了一个模拟疏散基准框架，测试两种沟通策略（窄播vs广播）、两种环境表征（视觉vs图结构）和两种威胁行为（静态vs移动），在9张不同复杂度的地图上评估VLMs引导平民的表现。
AI.result: 窄播策略在所有难度级别上持续降低平民失败率；视觉模态是性能的主要驱动因素，而添加邻接图的效果依赖模型且通常有害；移动威胁在所有条件下均提高失败率。
AI.conclusion: 部署VLMs作为疏散场景中的AI操作员仍是非平凡挑战，沟通策略和输入表示的选择直接决定干预的成功或失败。

title: Self-Explainability in Self-Adaptive and Self-Organising Systems: Status and Research Directions
comment: Under review as a regular paper at ACM Transactions on Autonomous and Adaptive Systems (TAAS)
summary: The growing complexity of self-adaptive and self-organising systems, fuelled by advances in Artificial Intelligence (AI), has made them increasingly difficult to understand and trust. While Explainable AI aims to provide insight into AI decision-making, a more advanced goal is for systems to explain themselves - an ability referred to as Self-Explainability (SX). This article presents a systematic literature review on SX, analysing existing approaches, including their domains, targets, and evaluation methods. The review develops a unified definition and taxonomy of SX and introduces Levels of Self-Explainability, providing a framework for positioning current and future research. Our results show that most SX approaches remain conceptual, with few practical implementations. Moreover, there is currently no formal or de facto standard for evaluating SX, highlighting a major research gap. This work thus establishes a foundation and roadmap for advancing Self-Explainability in complex systems.

AI.tldr: 本文对自我解释性（Self-Explainability, SX）进行了系统性文献综述，提出了统一的定义、分类法和"自我解释性级别"框架。研究发现大多数SX方法仍停留在概念阶段，缺乏实际实现，且尚无评估标准，为未来研究奠定了基础和路线图。
AI.motivation: 随着AI驱动的自适应和自组织系统日益复杂，它们变得难以理解和信任。虽然可解释AI（XAI）旨在提供AI决策的洞见，但更高级的目标是让系统能够自我解释（Self-Explainability），然而该领域缺乏统一的定义和系统性的梳理。
AI.method: 采用系统性文献综述方法，对现有的SX方法进行分析，包括它们应用的领域、目标和评估方法。在此基础上开发了统一的定义和分类法，并引入了"自我解释性级别"（Levels of Self-Explainability）框架。
AI.result: 大多数SX方法仍停留在概念层面，缺乏实际实现；目前没有正式或事实上的SX评估标准，这是一个重大的研究空白。
AI.conclusion: 本文为推进复杂系统中的自我解释性建立了基础和路线图，指出了当前研究的不足和未来方向。

title: MUDIDI: A Two-Stage Framework for Multilingual Dictionary Digitization with Language Models
summary: Multilingual dictionaries are among the most valuable documentary resources for low-resource and endangered languages, yet many remain available only as scans. For many decades, their digitization and conversion into a machine-readable format was nearly impossible due to language-specific scripts, complex multi-column layouts full of entries with abbreviations and cross-references. Recent vision-language models offer a promising solution, but it is unclear how well they preserve characters, markup, and process lexicographic structure. We introduce MUDIDI, a two-stage framework for multi-lingual dictionary digitization. Stage One evaluates the quality of character recognition and markup preservation; Stage Two focuses on dictionary entry segmentation with subsequent mapping into a machine-readable lexicographic schema, SIL's Multi-Dictionary Formatter. We also release a dataset that consists of human-annotated lexicographic entries collected from 30 public-domain dictionaries featuring diverse writing systems, language families, and formats. We benchmark OCR systems, general-purpose Large Language Models (LLMs), and Vision Language Models (VLMs) on the dataset, demonstrating superior performance of LLMs across most writing systems and languages in both stages, and provide practical guidelines on improving the results for more challenging scenarios. Finally, we show that supplementing additional information, such as dictionary introduction, to the LLMs can improve the quality of the digitized dictionary. Github: https://github.com/DavidSamuell/MUDIDI-Pipeline-for-Digitization-of-Multilingual-Dictionary/

AI.tldr: 本文提出一个名为 MUDIDI 的两阶段多语词典数字化框架：第一阶段评估字符识别与标记保留质量，第二阶段进行词典条目分割并映射为机器可读的词典格式。作者构建了包含30种公共领域词典的人工标注数据集，并系统比较了OCR、通用大语言模型（LLM）和视觉语言模型（VLM）的性能，发现LLM在多数文字系统中表现更优。此外，向LLM补充词典前言等额外信息能进一步提高数字化质量。
AI.motivation: 多语词典是低资源语言和濒危语言的重要文献资源，但许多词典仅有扫描版，因复杂的多栏版式、语言特定文字、缩写及交叉引用，传统数字化方法（如OCR）长年难以将其转化为机器可读格式。最近出现的视觉语言模型展示了潜力，但尚不清楚它们在保真字符、标记和保留词典学结构方面的实际能力，亟需系统评估与标准化框架。
AI.method: 提出MUDIDI两阶段框架：Stage 1评估字符识别与标记保留质量；Stage 2进行词典条目分割并映射到SIL的Multi-Dictionary Formatter（MDF）机器可读词典学模式。构建包含30部公共领域多语词典的人工标注数据集，覆盖多种书写系统、语言家族和版式。在此数据集上对OCR系统、通用LLM和VLM进行基准测试。
AI.result: 在多数书写系统和语言中，LLM（通用大语言模型）在Stage 1（字符/标记保留）和Stage 2（条目分割与结构映射）两个阶段均显著优于OCR系统和VLM；补充词典前言等额外信息给LLM可进一步提升数字化质量。
AI.conclusion: 对于复杂多语词典的数字化，基于LLM的方法比传统OCR和当前VLM更为有效；通过提供词典元信息（如前言）可进一步改善结果。研究为低资源和濒危语言词典的数字化提供了实用指南和开源工具。

title: Provably Efficient Personalized Multi-Objective Bandits with Proactive Conversational Queries
summary: Personalized decision-making in multi-objective bandits requires learning user-specific trade-offs among competing objectives. Since arm utility depends on both unknown rewards and unknown preferences, existing methods infer preferences only from utility feedback, entangling preference learning with reward exploration. In practice, however, users often reveal their priorities through proactive conversational queries (e.g., "cheap and clean hotel"), yet this structured signal is not leveraged. We formalize a proactive query-based framework in which user queries provide structured preference signals. Modeling these signals via a Plackett-Luce subset choice model, we show that query-only learning is insufficient due to a fundamental shift-invariance barrier. To resolve this, we introduce MO-PQUCB, a hybrid algorithm that integrates query-based preference anchoring with bandit feedback through shift-invariant regularization and dual-exploration UCB. We prove that proactive queries accelerate preference estimation and yield improved regret scaling over prior preference-aware MO-MAB methods. Under corrupted queries, we further characterize statistical limits and design a robust estimator achieving near-optimal performance when the corruption is sparse. Experiments validate both theoretical and practical gains.

AI.tldr: 本文提出MO-PQUCB算法，通过融合用户主动查询（如"便宜且干净的酒店"）中的结构化偏好信号与多目标多臂老虎机反馈，解决了仅靠查询学习存在移位不变性障碍的问题，证明了主动查询能加速偏好估计并提升遗憾界，同时设计了针对查询腐败的鲁棒估计器。
AI.motivation: 现有多目标多臂老虎机方法仅从效用反馈中推断用户偏好，将偏好学习与奖励探索纠缠在一起。然而实际中用户常通过主动对话查询（如"便宜且干净的酒店"）透露优先级，这种结构化信号未被充分利用。
AI.method: 形式化了一个基于主动查询的框架，使用Plackett-Luce子集选择模型建模查询信号；提出MO-PQUCB混合算法，通过移位不变正则化和双探索UCB整合基于查询的偏好锚定与老虎机反馈；并设计针对腐败查询的鲁棒估计器。
AI.result: 理论证明主动查询可加速偏好估计，并优于先前偏好感知的多目标多臂老虎机方法的遗憾界；在查询腐败场景下刻画了统计极限，稀疏腐败时鲁棒估计器达到近乎最优性能；实验验证了理论和实践优势。
AI.conclusion: 主动查询信号能有效解耦偏好学习与奖励探索，提升多目标个性化决策的效率与鲁棒性。

title: TABVERSE: Benchmarking Cross-Format Table Understanding in LLMs and VLMs
comment: 24 pages, 18 tables, 16 figures, Submitted to ARR May 2026
summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) and Vision-Language Models (VLMs) are increasingly evaluated on table reasoning tasks, but the role of table representation remains under-explored. In practice, the same table content may appear in different structural formats, such as HTML, Markdown, and LaTeX, or as rendered images. However, existing evaluations often let content, format, layout, and modality vary together, making it difficult to isolate representation effects. We introduce TABVERSE, a controlled multimodal table benchmark that aligns the same table content across multiple structural formats and rendered images, with question category and difficulty tags. This design enables systematic evaluation of representation effects while holding table content fixed. We evaluate LLMs and VLMs across three tasks: Question Answering (QA), Structural Understanding Capability (SUC), and Structure Reconstruction (SR). Our results show that representation choice substantially affects table understanding. Models generally perform better with structured text than with rendered images, but the size of this gap depends on the task, model, and format. HTML is often the most robust text format, while row-sensitive structural tasks and syntactically usable LaTeX reconstruction remain challenging. These findings show that table representation is a key factor in reliable table evaluation.

AI.tldr: 本文提出TABVERSE基准，通过控制表格内容不变、变化结构格式（HTML/Markdown/LaTeX）和渲染图像，系统评估了表示形式对LLM和VLM表格理解的影响，发现表示选择显著影响性能，HTML最鲁棒，而图像和LaTeX仍存在挑战。
AI.motivation: 现有表格推理评测中，表格内容、格式、布局和模态往往同时变化，难以隔离表示形式本身的效应，因此需要构建一个可控的多模态表格基准来单独研究表示选择的影响。
AI.method: 构建TABVERSE基准，将相同表格内容对齐到多种结构格式（HTML、Markdown、LaTeX）和渲染图像中，并附加问题类别与难度标签。在QA、结构理解能力（SUC）和结构重建（SR）三个任务上评估LLM和VLM。
AI.result: 表示选择显著影响表格理解性能；结构化文本普遍优于渲染图像，但差距大小取决于任务、模型和格式；HTML是最鲁棒的文本格式；行敏感的结构任务和LaTeX重建仍具有挑战性。
AI.conclusion: 表格表示形式是可靠表格评估中的关键因素，未来评测应关注表示效应，并注意不同格式带来的性能差异。

title: Reasoning without Gold Standards: A Proxy-Judge Theory of Autoformalization
summary:
Complex reasoning tasks increasingly require systems to produce outputs whose correctness cannot be judged by exact match against a single reference. Autoformalization (AF) is a representative example; it asks a model to translate informal mathematical or logical reasoning into a formally checkable object, yet expert-validated formalizations do not scale beyond toy cases and a single informal argument can admit many valid formal renderings. Progress therefore depends on whether partial, structured proxies can substitute for exact references.
  We introduce a reference-free proxy-judge framework for AF that replaces gold-standard matching with a vector of per-axis property checks. The framework organizes the proxy along three structural scopes that cover global properties of the elicited object, per-module properties internal to its sub-components, and cross-domain properties that re-align it to the informal source, and aggregates each axis into a verdict vector. The vector drives a reflective refinement loop in which a violated coordinate routes the controller to a matching repair target, so each iteration changes only what is judged wrong.
  Under bounded judge noise, the expected intrinsic gap contracts geometrically to a noise-dependent plateau. Across seven formalization backbones on miniF2F, ProofNet, e-SNLI, and ProntoQA, refinement consistently lifts Pass Rate over the single-shot ICL baseline, and the per-axis proxy outperforms a matched scalar proxy on benchmarks where the baseline has room to improve. Structured proxy judgments therefore provide both a practical refinement signal and a theoretical handle on convergence when exact references are unavailable.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种无需参考标准的代理评判框架，通过多轴属性检查替代精确匹配，用于自动形式化（AF）任务的迭代优化，并在多个基准上显著提升了通过率。
AI.motivation: 复杂推理任务（如自动形式化）中，精确匹配单一参考标准不可行，因为同一非正式论证可对应多种有效的形式化表达，且专家验证无法规模化，因此需要无需参考标准的代理评判方法。
AI.method: 提出参考无关的代理评判框架，沿三个结构范围（全局属性、模块内属性、跨域属性）组织代理评判，将每个轴聚合成判决向量，驱动反思性细化循环：被违反的坐标路由控制器到对应修复目标，每次仅更改被判错的部分。
AI.result: 在有界评判噪声下，期望内在差距几何收敛到噪声依赖的平台。在miniF2F、ProofNet、e-SNLI、ProntoQA上，使用七个形式化骨干网络，细化持续提升单次ICL基线的通过率，且多轴代理在基线有改进空间的基准上优于标量代理。
AI.conclusion: 结构化代理评判既提供了实用的细化信号，又在无精确参考时提供了理论上的收敛保证。

title: Sparrow: Sparse Rollout for Stable and Efficient Long-context RL of Large Language Models
summary: Despite being powerful, reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) induces extremely long COT, making it computationally expensive. Since RLVR per-step cost is dominated by long-context rollout generation, sparse attention offers a promising way to accelerate dense rollout. However, sparse rollouts require a delicate stability-efficiency tradeoff: overly aggressive sparsity causes collapse, while overly lenient sparsity gives insufficient speedup. In this work, we study this tradeoff through sparse-to-dense actor-policy mismatch. We first observe that sparse rollout collapse is not driven by uniform degradation across tokens: most sparse tokens align perfectly with dense even under aggressive sparsity. Motivated by this, we hypothesize that sparse rollout training remains stable if the lower tail of per-token actor-policy mismatch stays above a critical threshold throughout the trajectory. We introduce a dynamic sparsity schedule that keeps this tail statistic constant during generation and validate our hypothesis. Across Qwen3 thinking-family models, keeping the tail mismatch statistic near a consistent threshold generally enables stable training. We then use a cost model to find the sparsity schedule for maximum speedup under this mismatch threshold, achieving 2.2x, 2.4x, and 2.0x rollout speedups when training Qwen3-1.7B, Qwen3-4B, and Qwen3-8B. Empirically, we show the thresholds generalize to a larger model (Qwen3-14B) and another RL domain (coding). Finally, our analysis naturally motivates DistillSparse: lightweight LoRA-based distillation on sparse rollout lets more aggressive sparsity reach the same sparse-to-dense mismatch threshold, yielding higher speedup.

AI.tldr: 针对RLVR中稀疏注意力加速rollout时存在的稳定性-效率权衡问题，本文发现稀疏rollout崩溃并非由所有token均匀退化导致，而是由actor-policy mismatch的尾部token决定。提出动态稀疏调度策略，通过保持每token mismatch尾部统计量在临界阈值以上来保证稳定训练，在Qwen3系列模型上实现2.0-2.4倍rollout加速。进一步提出DistillSparse，通过轻量LoRA蒸馏实现更激进稀疏下的更高加速。
AI.motivation: RLVR虽然强大，但会诱导极长的COT（思维链），导致计算成本高昂。由于RLVR的每步成本主要由长上下文rollout生成主导，稀疏注意力是加速密集rollout的有效途径，但存在稳定性-效率权衡：过于激进的稀疏会导致训练崩溃，而过于保守的稀疏则加速不足。本文旨在研究并解决这一权衡问题。
AI.method: 1. 观察到稀疏rollout崩溃并非由所有token的均匀退化导致，大多数稀疏token即使在激进稀疏下也能与密集版本对齐良好。2. 提出假设：若整个轨迹中每token的actor-policy mismatch的尾部（低分位数）保持在临界阈值以上，则稀疏rollout训练保持稳定。3. 引入动态稀疏调度，在生成过程中保持该尾部统计量恒定。4. 通过成本模型找到在该mismatch阈值下实现最大加速的稀疏调度。5. 提出DistillSparse：基于轻量LoRA的蒸馏方法，使更激进的稀疏能达到相同的mismatch阈值，从而获得更高加速。
AI.result: 在Qwen3-1.7B、Qwen3-4B和Qwen3-8B上分别实现了2.2倍、2.4倍和2.0倍的rollout加速。阈值泛化性验证：在更大模型Qwen3-14B和另一个RL领域（代码）上同样有效。
AI.conclusion: 尾部mismatch统计量是控制稀疏rollout稳定性的关键指标；动态稀疏调度可以在此约束下实现高效加速；DistillSparse通过蒸馏进一步提升了加速效果。

title: Optical Reasoning: Rethinking Images as an Expressive Reasoning Medium Beyond Text
summary: Chain-of-Thought (CoT) improves the performance of Large Language Models (LLMs) and has been extended to Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs). More recent work further moves from text-based multimodal reasoning toward interleaved-modal reasoning, where intermediate steps can incorporate both textual rationales and visual evidence. In this work, we propose a bolder and more ambitious idea: could images alone serve as the reasoning medium for both language and multimodal tasks? To explore this, we propose optical reasoning, which treats images as a standalone reasoning medium. We instantiate this concept with two variants: typographic-based optical reasoning, which optimizes visual layouts for compact rationale rendering, and graphical-based optical reasoning, which composes text and graphical elements into structured visual rationales. Across mathematical, scientific, and interleaved-modal reasoning benchmarks, optical reasoning can match or even exceed traditional text reasoning while reducing reasoning tokens by an average of 28.57% on language tasks and 16% on multimodal tasks, achieving 1.96 times the token efficiency of text reasoning. These results show that images can effectively and efficiently encode rationales while providing a unified visual canvas for reasoning.

AI.tldr: 本文提出"光学推理"(Optical Reasoning)概念，探索仅用图像作为推理媒介，在数学、科学和多模态推理任务中，性能匹配或超越传统文本推理，同时减少28.57%和16%的推理token，token效率提升1.96倍。
AI.motivation: 现有CoT推理从纯文本推理发展到交错模态推理（文本+视觉），本文进一步提出更大胆的想法：能否仅用图像作为推理媒介，统一处理语言和多模态任务？
AI.method: 提出两种光学推理变体：(1) 基于排印的光学推理——优化视觉布局以紧凑呈现推理过程；(2) 基于图形的光学推理——将文本和图形元素组合成结构化视觉推理过程。
AI.result: 在数学、科学和交错模态推理基准上，光学推理匹配或超越传统文本推理，在语言任务上减少28.57%推理token，在多模态任务上减少16%，token效率达到文本推理的1.96倍。
AI.conclusion: 图像能够有效且高效地编码推理过程，并提供一个统一的视觉推理画布。

title: AbstRAG: Learning to Abstract for Retrieval Problems
summary: Retrieval-augmented generation often fails when the query, the document evidence, and the user's intent are expressed at different levels of abstraction. A query may ask about a class, a relation, or an event, while the document only states specific instances, indirect framings, or scoped formulations. We define this mismatch as an abstraction gap: the minimal set of typed assumptions required to align query intent with the available evidence. To close this gap, we introduce AbstRAG, which treats abstraction as an explicit retrieval object. AbstRAG decomposes the query--evidence gap into expression, conceptual, intent--evidence, and event-type components, and scores relevance by combining match quality, a query-independent utility prior, and the cost of the required bridges. Its central mechanism is reflective refinement: a critic diagnoses retrieval failures, localizes the failed abstraction operator, proposes a minimal stage-specific patch, and accepts the patch only under sufficiency and compression controls. Across three within-document retrieval benchmarks against seven baselines, AbstRAG outperforms on nDCG@10 in 18 of 21 paired-bootstrap contrasts and improves generation accuracy by 1.9%, 5.2%, and 4.0% across the three benchmarks; ablations confirm that reflective refinement drives most of the retrieval gain and the compression control alone reduces over-expansion false positives from 73.7% to 0% on a stress slice.

AI.tldr: AbstRAG通过将"抽象鸿沟"显式建模为检索对象，并引入反思性精炼机制，在查询与文档证据存在抽象层次不匹配时显著提升了检索增强生成的效果。
AI.motivation: 现有检索增强生成系统在查询、文档证据与用户意图处于不同抽象层级时经常失败，例如查询针对类别而文档只提及具体实例。作者将此问题定义为"抽象鸿沟"，并旨在通过显式建模来弥合这一鸿沟。
AI.method: AbstRAG将抽象本身视为显式检索对象，将查询-证据鸿沟分解为表达、概念、意图-证据和事件类型四个组件，通过组合匹配质量、查询无关的效用先验和所需桥梁成本来评分相关性。核心机制是反思性精炼：批评者诊断检索失败，定位失败的抽象算子，提出最小阶段级补丁，并在充分性和压缩控制下接受补丁。
AI.result: 在三个文档内检索基准上，AbstRAG在21个配对bootstrap对比中18个指标的nDCG@10上超越七个基线；生成准确率分别提升1.9%、5.2%和4.0%；消融实验确认反思性精炼驱动了大部分检索增益，压缩控制单独将过度扩展误报从73.7%降至0%。
AI.conclusion: AbstRAG通过显式建模抽象鸿沟并引入反思性精炼机制，有效解决了检索增强生成中的抽象层次不匹配问题，验证了将抽象作为检索对象的有效性。

title: Not Just After One: Sleep-Inspired Replay Prevents Catastrophic Forgetting After Sequential Tasks
summary: One of the critical limitations of artificial neural networks is their lack of ability to continually learn: training on new tasks often leads to interference and forgetting of the previous ones. While several algorithms have been proposed to protect old memories from interference, they are typically applied during or immediately after each new episode of training. In contrast, humans and animals can learn continuously, acquiring multiple new memories during active learning before consolidating all of them into long-term storage. Here we show that multiple new tasks can be trained sequentially before an unsupervised sleep-like replay phase is applied to partially restore performance across all previously learned tasks. Our study further suggests that task-specific information remains resilient to new training but decays gradually as network is trained on new tasks. These findings point to novel principles for developing a broad range of continual learning AI solutions.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种受生物启发的持续学习方法，允许神经网络在多个新任务上顺序训练后，通过无监督的类睡眠回放阶段来部分恢复所有先前学习任务的性能，而无需在每个新任务后立即进行记忆保护。
AI.motivation: 解决人工神经网络在持续学习中的灾难性遗忘问题，模仿人类和动物"先主动学习多个新任务，再通过睡眠-like过程进行记忆巩固"的学习模式。
AI.method: 允许神经网络顺序训练多个新任务，然后在无监督的类睡眠回放阶段中，通过重放过去经历的样本来部分恢复所有先前学习任务的性能。
AI.result: 多个新任务可以顺序训练，之后通过类睡眠回放阶段有效恢复之前任务的性能；任务特定信息对新训练具有一定韧性，但会随着网络在新任务上的训练而逐渐衰减。
AI.conclusion: 这些发现为开发更广泛的持续学习AI解决方案提供了新的原则，即利用类似睡眠的记忆巩固机制来实现更自然、更高效的持续学习。

title: Next-Token Prediction Learns Generalisable Representations of Sleep Physiology
summary: Foundation models offer a promising route to compress multi-modal physiological signals into compact representations of human health, with broad applications across sleep medicine, cardiology, neurology and other healthcare domains. Existing models have typically been trained with masked-reconstruction or contrastive objectives. However, masked reconstruction may be poorly suited to the stochastic nature of these signals, while contrastive approaches rely on positive-pair definitions despite the semantic invariances of physiological signals being poorly understood. In this work, we show that next-token prediction is a simple and scalable alternative. We develop Hypnos, a multi-modal sleep foundation model trained using eight different sensing modalities (e.g. EEG, ECG, respiratory signals) drawn from over 20,000 overnight polysomnography recordings. We tokenize each modality into streams of discrete tokens using residual vector quantization, then train a large auto-regressive RQ-Transformer to jointly predict the next token across all modalities in parallel. After training, Hypnos can be applied to continuous streams of sensor data from any subset of supported modalities, generating embeddings for downstream tasks. Across a range of benchmarks, Hypnos significantly outperforms existing foundation models. In sleep stage classification, we match the performance of strong supervised baselines on held-out test sets whilst using \(100\times\) less labelled data. Hypnos even generalises to daytime physiology, surpassing a dedicated ECG foundation model at detecting atrial fibrillation. Our results demonstrate that next-token prediction is a strong self-supervised objective for representation learning from multi-modal physiological signals.

AI.tldr: 本文提出Hypnos，一种基于下一个token预测的多模态睡眠基础模型，在8种传感模态上训练，显著优于现有基础模型，在睡眠分期任务中仅用1%标签数据即可媲美强监督基线。
AI.motivation: 现有基础模型使用掩码重建或对比学习训练，但掩码重建不适合生理信号的随机性，对比学习依赖难以定义的正样本对，因此需要更合适的自监督目标。
AI.method: 使用残差向量量化将8种生理信号模态（EEG、ECG、呼吸信号等）转化为离散token流，训练自回归RQ-Transformer并行预测所有模态的下一个token。
AI.result: Hypnos在多个基准上显著优于现有基础模型；睡眠分期任务中用1%标签数据达到强监督基线性能；泛化到日间生理数据，在房颤检测上超越专用ECG基础模型。
AI.conclusion: 下一个token预测是学习多模态生理信号表示的一种强大的自监督目标。

title: H2HMem: A Multimodal Memory Benchmark for Agents in Human-Human Interactions
summary: Large language model agents are increasingly deployed in human-human interaction settings, such as meeting assistants and clinical documentation systems, where they must observe conversations and retain information for downstream queries. Unlike traditional human-assistant settings, these environments are inherently multimodal, involve complex discourse phenomena such as anaphora and deixis, and contain asynchronous or conflicting information from multiple participants. However, existing memory benchmarks largely focus on single-user, text-only interactions, failing to capture these challenges. To address this gap, we introduce H2HMem, a Human-to-Human Multimodal Memory Benchmark for evaluating memory capabilities in complex human-human interactions. H2HMem includes both dyadic and multi-party conversations with multimodal information streams, and evaluates agents along three dimensions: memory recall, reasoning, and application. Experiments with advanced agents reveal substantial limitations in constructing, retaining, and utilizing memories across modalities, participants, and sessions, highlighting substantial room for improvement in next-generation LLM agents.

AI.tldr: 本文提出H2HMem，一个面向人-人多模态交互场景的大语言模型智能体记忆评估基准，实验发现当前先进智能体在多模态、多参与者、多会话的记忆构建、保留和利用方面存在显著局限。
AI.motivation: 现有记忆基准主要聚焦于单用户纯文本交互，无法覆盖人-人交互场景中的多模态性、复杂话语现象（如照应、指代）以及多参与者的异步/冲突信息，亟需一个新的评估基准来填补这一空白。
AI.method: 构建了包含双人对话和多方对话的多模态信息流数据集，从记忆回忆、推理和应用三个维度评估智能体的记忆能力。
AI.result: 当前先进智能体在跨模态、跨参与者、跨会话的记忆构建、保留和利用上表现显著不足，存在大量改进空间。
AI.conclusion: 人-人多模态交互场景对LLM智能体的记忆能力提出了远超现有基准的挑战，下一代智能体需要在多模态记忆构建与跨会话推理方面进行实质改进。

title: Theoretical Foundations of Continual Learning via Drift-Plus-Penalty
comment: Accepted to Transactions on Machine Learning Research (TMLR)
summary: In many real-world settings, data streams are nonstationary and arrive sequentially, requiring learning systems to adapt continuously without retraining from scratch. Continual learning (CL) addresses this challenge by incorporating new tasks while mitigating catastrophic forgetting, where learning new information degrades performance on previously acquired knowledge. We introduce a control-theoretic perspective on CL that explicitly regulates the evolution of forgetting, framing adaptation as a controlled process subject to long-term stability constraints. We focus on replay-based CL, where a finite memory buffer stores representative samples from prior tasks. We propose COntinual Learning with Drift-Plus-Penalty (COLD), a continual learning framework based on the Drift-Plus-Penalty (DPP) principle from stochastic optimization. To facilitate analysis, we also consider an oracle variant, COLD-ORACLE, as a reference benchmark. At each task, both methods minimize the current task loss while maintaining a virtual queue that tracks deviations from long-term stability on previously learned tasks, capturing the stability-plasticity trade-off as a regulated dynamical process. We establish stability and convergence guarantees that characterize this trade-off through a tunable control parameter.
